summary: The Chinese Civil War started when I was just two years old, around 1927. It was a conflict between the Kuomintang and the Communist Party of China. This war shaped much of my early life and the history of China during that time. It was a turbulent period that eventually led to major changes in the country.

The Kuomintang, or KMT, was one of the main forces in the Chinese Civil War. They were fighting against the Communists for control of China. In 1949, when the Communists gained ground, the KMT forces, including myself, retreated to Taiwan to regroup. The KMT's escape to Taiwan marked a significant turning point in my life and the history of China.

The Communist Party of China was the other major side in the Chinese Civil War, fighting against the Kuomintang. Around 1949, they gained a lot of ground and took control of mainland China. This forced many of us, including the KMT forces, to flee to Taiwan. The Communists' rise changed the political landscape dramatically during my youth.

During my teenage years, around 1940, the Empire of Japan was at war with the Communists in China. This conflict directly affected my life when they tried to conscript me into the military. My father stood up to block them, but after that, he was killed—though I'm not sure if it was by the Japanese or the Communists. The Japanese occupation was a dangerous and uncertain time for my family.

The year 1949 was a turning point in my life and in Chinese history. That was when the Communists gained significant control over China, and the Kuomintang forces, including myself, fled to Taiwan. It was a time of upheaval and change, marking the end of one chapter and the beginning of another in Taiwan. This move shaped my future and the community I became part of.

After 1949, I came to Taiwan with the Kuomintang forces who had escaped there. Most of the people working in restaurants at that time were from the Mainland, like me. I apprenticed in restaurants and learned skills like roasting duck. Taiwan became my new home and the place where I built my life after leaving China.
